prompt: Write a CloudEvent consumer/parser in Python that can handle both HTTP binary mode and structured mode CloudEvents. It should detect which mode was used by inspecting the Content-Type header, extract all context attributes, and return a parsed CloudEvent dict. Handle both JSON data and binary (base64) data payloads. Include error handling for invalid events.
assertions: Content mode detection checks for 'application/cloudevents-batch' prefix BEFORE or separately from 'application/cloudevents' prefix (to avoid batch being misdetected as structured)
Binary mode reads ce- prefixed headers to extract attributes (contains code that strips 'ce-' prefix from header names)
In binary mode the Content-Type header value becomes the datacontenttype (no ce-datacontenttype header used)
The code handles data_base64 by performing base64 decoding (imports base64 module or uses b64decode)
Validates all four required attributes are present: specversion, id, source, type (checks for each one)
The code performs percent-decoding (urllib.parse.unquote or equivalent) on ce- header values
Extension attributes from unknown ce- headers are preserved in the parsed output (not discarded)
